questions.title: CBT Session: Opening Joke
questions.scenario: During the homework review regarding 'should' statements, what specific 'bad therapy joke' did the therapist make?

mustInclude: The therapist makes a pun on 'should' sounding like 'sh*t' (i.e., 'should all over yourself')
The joke is phrased as a warning/caution (e.g., 'be careful')
mustAvoid: A joke that does not include the 'should all over yourself' pun
A generic comment about stopping 'shoulds' without the pun
Inventing a different joke (e.g., about traffic signs, perfectionism) not in the transcript

questions.title: CBT Session: Situation Analysis
questions.scenario: In the specific situation discussed (the phone call), at what point in the conversation did the client feel they were 'not ready'?

mustInclude: It happened in the middle of the conversation
It happened when the friend shifted to checking in on her / asking how she was doing / asking about her
mustAvoid: Saying it was at the beginning of the call
Saying it was when he suggested coffee (she was initially okay with that)
Saying it was simply because he called (timing needs the mid-call shift)

questions.title: CBT Session: Metaphor Analysis
questions.scenario: The client introduces a 'stop sign' metaphor regarding the interaction with her friend. According to the transcript, explicitly explain the 'two ways' this stop sign operates.

mustInclude: It stops the client from sharing/telling how she is really doing (e.g., depressed/anxious/'bad')
It stops (or is meant to stop) the friend from asking/checking in (i.e., 'don't ask')
The client believes people aren't ready to hear the truth about how bad she feels
It reflects mutual avoidance/keeping things quiet ("you don't ask and I'm not going to tell")
mustAvoid: Interpreting it as literal traffic rules
Explaining it only as generic boundary-setting without the 'don't ask / don't tell' dynamic
Saying it just means 'stop talking' without specifying both directions

questions.title: CBT Session: Judge Analogy
questions.scenario: When introducing the concept of putting thoughts on trial, how did the therapist describe Judge Judy's personality? List at least two specific descriptors used.

mustInclude: At least two of the following: strong personality; not wishy-washy; not gentle; a little mean; a little gruff
The description is tied to being tough/strict about evidence (not accepting weak evidence)
mustAvoid: Claiming the therapist described her as 'fair' or 'unbiased' (not stated)
Adding unrelated traits not mentioned (e.g., 'loud', 'intelligent') as if they were in the transcript

questions.title: CBT Session: Evidence Ratings
questions.scenario: During the evidence gathering phase, the client and therapist assigned strength ratings (1-3). What rating was assigned to the evidence that 'listening to complaining is annoying', and what rating was assigned to the fact that the friend is a 'support worker'?

mustInclude: Listening to complaining is annoying: rated 3 (client says 'almost like a 3')
Friend being a support worker: rated 3
mustAvoid: Swapping the ratings
Giving non-numeric/unspecified ratings (the question asks for the numbers)
Claiming either was rated 2

questions.title: CBT Session: Evidence Reasoning
questions.scenario: One piece of 'Evidence Against' involved the friend's discretion. How did the client rate the strength of the evidence that he 'hasn't gossiped about her in the past,' and what was the specific reasoning for that low rating?

mustInclude: Rating: 1
Reason: she isn't sure / 'not that I'm aware' / they don't actually know for certain
mustAvoid: Rating: 2 or 3
Reasoning framed as certainty (e.g., 'he would never gossip') rather than uncertainty

questions.title: CBT Session: Balanced Thought Phrasing
questions.scenario: Construct the final 'Balanced Thought' exactly as agreed upon by the client and therapist. It follows the format 'It may be true that... and it is also true that...'

mustInclude: It may be true that I'm scared of bothering people
It is also true that I have good friends who can and do support me
Must include the wording 'can and do support me' (not just 'can support me')
mustAvoid: Using the original hot thought as the final balanced thought (e.g., 'I shouldn't be bothering him')
Replacing the balanced thought with a different summary like 'I'm not alone' (even if consistent, it's not the phrasing agreed upon)
Paraphrasing that drops the 'can and do' refinement

questions.title: CBT Session: Emotional Shifts
questions.scenario: Compare the client's self-rated Anxiety and Fear levels (0-100%) regarding the 'Hot Thought' before the exercise versus after the thought was restructured. State the specific start and end numbers for both.

mustInclude: Anxiety: 40% before, 20% after
Fear: 60% before, 30% after
mustAvoid: Mixing up anxiety and fear values
Providing only approximate values (e.g., 'about half') instead of the stated numbers
Reporting only 'before' or only 'after' values

questions.title: CBT Session: Physical Symptoms
questions.scenario: For the homework experiment prediction, what specific physical sensations did the client identify as indicators that her anxiety is rising?

mustInclude: Knot in stomach
Shaky / shakiness
mustAvoid: Symptoms not mentioned (e.g., sweating, rapid heartbeat, headache)
Describing it as a panic attack (she explicitly contrasts it with not having a panic attack)

questions.title: CBT Session: Obstacle Management
questions.scenario: The client identified 'being too anxious to reach out' as a potential obstacle to the homework. What specific coping strategy did they agree to write down on the plan to overcome this?

mustInclude: Take a picture/photo (of the whiteboard/thought record/evidence)
Use/look at the picture as a reminder if anxiety gets high
mustAvoid: Coping strategies not agreed on in the transcript (e.g., deep breathing, calling the therapist, postponing indefinitely)
Vague answers like 'just try harder' without the concrete 'picture' strategy

questions.title: Strixhaven: Rootie's Misfire in the Tavern Fight
questions.scenario: When the mage-hunters crash into Bow’s End Tavern and Rootie immediately tries a big spell, what improvised casting focus does he pull from his bag, what goes wrong with the focus itself, and where does the spell end up going instead of hitting the target?

mustInclude: It was a parsnip carved into a wand (an improvised vegetable wand/focus)
The top/tip of the parsnip snaps off
The shot misses and goes into/over toward the corner (starting a fire is acceptable but not required)
mustAvoid: A generic wand/staff focus
He successfully hits the mage-hunter
It was a potato

questions.title: Strixhaven: Paddles' Delay and Snack
questions.scenario: After Abraham gets suplexed by Tulk and is lying dead, what two things does Paddles do before reviving him: (1) how does he position himself while waiting, and (2) what does he eat (include the detail about what he does to it right before eating)?

mustInclude: He sits crisscross applesauce on Abraham’s corpse/body while waiting
He eats a snack cake
He wipes extra blood from Abraham onto the snack cake (or otherwise smears blood on it) before eating
mustAvoid: Apple
Bread
Coffee/latte

questions.title: Strixhaven: Reconstructing the Psychic Draconic Message
questions.scenario: When Sigrid tries to reproduce the psychic Draconic she heard, what check does the DM call for (including the initial disadvantage), what specific help is offered to change that disadvantage, and what three elements does the DM say the sound resembles through the 'growling static'?

mustInclude: It starts as a Performance check with disadvantage
Conflegra provides beatboxing as bardic inspiration/help (beatboxing is explicitly part of it)
Abraham (who speaks Draconic) offers help by explaining Draconic phonemes/structure, changing it to advantage
The three descriptors: beatboxing, a bad French accent, and a dog chewing on a toy
mustAvoid: It was a History check
The sound is described only as 'Draconic language' without the three-part comparison
Singing as one of the three elements

questions.title: Strixhaven: Dean Tullus' Two-Level Threat
questions.scenario: Dean Augusta Tullus makes two separate threats in her rant at the Fire Jolt scene: one about the students responsible for the fire, and a second, more absurd one tied to coffee. What are both threats, and what is the five-minute condition attached to the coffee threat?

mustInclude: First threat: the responsible students will be expelled (and exiled from the magical community forever / 'worse than dead')
Second threat: if coffee doesn’t appear in five minutes she will kick everyone out of Lorehold so it will just be her
The five-minute condition is explicitly about coffee appearing
mustAvoid: She threatens to kill someone
She threatens to burn down the school
She threatens only the main characters (instead of a broader threat)

questions.title: Strixhaven: The 'Buffalo Change' Walk-By
questions.scenario: Near the stadium, Conflegra tap dances to impress people and calls out a specific step name. What step name does she vocalize, and what single-word response does the passing minotaur give?

mustInclude: She says 'Buffalo change'
The minotaur says 'Rude'
mustAvoid: The minotaur attacks her
The minotaur joins in
The minotaur ignores her entirely

questions.title: Strixhaven: Ruxa's Glasses—Mispronunciations and the Final Consensus
questions.scenario: In Bow’s End Tavern, Professor Ruxa (the bear) puts on a particular type of glasses. The DM and cast stumble over how to say it before settling on a repeated, wrong-sounding pronunciation. What eyewear is it, what are at least two of the DM’s attempted pronunciations before the group settles, and what final pronunciation do they repeat?

mustInclude: The glasses are pince-nez
At least two attempted pronunciations such as 'pin-nez', 'pin-wa', and/or 'pin-snez' are mentioned as the cast/DM fumble
The final repeated settled pronunciation is 'Pin-snez' (for pince-nez)
mustAvoid: Monocle
Regular spectacles/reading glasses without identifying pince-nez
Claiming they pronounce it correctly from the start

questions.title: Strixhaven: Rootie's Druidcraft 'Potato Harvest' from Muffy
questions.scenario: After the mage-hunter Muffy is killed, Rootie proposes using Druidcraft in a gross way. What crop does he specifically want to grow, and from what made-up magical organ/body part does he say he wants to grow it?

mustInclude: He wants to grow potatoes
He says 'counter spelling gland' (made-up organ/body part)
mustAvoid: Stomach
Heart
Brain

questions.title: Strixhaven: Abraham's 'Tell Me If I Smell' Request
questions.scenario: When Abraham speaks to Belladros Witherbloom, he makes a request about his own odor. What does he ask Belladros to do if he starts to smell like 'weed' or gets 'rank,' and what reason does he give that he won’t be insulted?

mustInclude: He asks Belladros to tell him / shout out (call it out) if he smells
He says he won’t be insulted because his nose hasn’t worked in years
mustAvoid: Belladros should clean him
Belladros should kill him
Belladros should ignore it

questions.title: Strixhaven: Egg-Cup vs. Evocation Shard Clarification
questions.scenario: While inspecting the blast site at the Fire Jolt table, the group finds a charred egg-cup-like holder and something rolls out of it. What rolls out, what kind of magic aura is it giving off, and what clarification does the DM make about what is magical versus what is not?

mustInclude: A curved glass piece/shard rolls out of the charred egg-cup/holder
It is giving off strong evocation
The DM clarifies the egg-cup is not magical; the glass/shard inside is the magical thing
mustAvoid: The egg-cup itself is the bomb/magic artifact
The egg-cup itself is magical

questions.title: Strixhaven: The Anime Theme Reference and the Pun
questions.scenario: Early on, the cast references an anime franchise theme song while talking about 'zaddies,' but they note they can’t sing it. What franchise do they reference, what short identifying phrase/line do they say about it (not the full song), and what pun does Aabria land on?

mustInclude: Digimon
They identify it as 'Digimon, digital monsters' (or equivalently mention 'digital monsters' as the identifier)
The pun is 'Digi-daddy'
mustAvoid: Pokemon
Dragonball Z
A generic 'daddy ending' answer without the Digi- pun
